Looking at the current image, I can see:
- The blue square is back below the gray box with green (at [34,37]x[8,11])
- The checkerboard moved back to [20,21]x[18,21] (above the red rectangle)
- The red rectangle is still horizontal at [30,33]x[12,17]
- The green square is at [38,39]x[8,9]
So clicking the blue shape toggles the blue square and checkerboard positions. Clicking the red shape toggles the red rectangle position.
Let me check the diff to confirm.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

before_ascii = before.ascii.split('\n')
after_ascii = after.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== DIFF ===")
for r in range(len(before_ascii)):
    if r < len(after_ascii) and before_ascii[r] != after_ascii[r]:
        for c in range(min(len(before_ascii[r]), len(after_ascii[r]))):
            if before_ascii[r][c] != after_ascii[r][c]:
                print(f"  Row {r}, Col {c}: '{before_ascii[r][c]}' -> '{after_ascii[r][c]}'")

# Check current objects
print("\n=== CURRENT LEFT PANEL OBJECTS ===")
for node in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] > 100:
        continue
    boundary = node['boundary']
    min_col = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
    max_col = max(p[1] for p in boundary)
    if max_col < 32 and node['pixels'] > 1:
        min_row = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        max_row = max(p[0] for p in boundary)
        print(f"  color={node['color']}, px={node['pixels']}, [{min_row},{max_row}]x[{min_col},{max_col}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- Clicking red shape: toggles red rectangle between [24,29]x[18,21] and [30,33]x[12,17]
